summary: File format validation (ALLOWED_EXTS) lives only in import.rs — recording path bypasses it entirely
failure_scenario: If drag-drop or another import vector is added later, it must manually reuse validate_source(). Recording uses native WAV via cpal/hound so format is implicit, but any future non-WAV recording format (e.g., Opus) would need shared validation.

summary: Recording uses UUID naming with hardcoded .wav extension; import preserves original extension
failure_scenario: Inconsistent naming conventions across entry points. If a meeting is created via recording then later has imported audio attached (or vice versa), file naming patterns diverge. No shared naming utility.

summary: audio_dir created once at startup in setup() — no runtime existence check before use
failure_scenario: If user deletes ~/Library/Application Support/meeting-ai/audio/ at runtime (or disk corruption), both import_meeting and start_recording will fail with cryptic filesystem errors instead of auto-recreating or surfacing a clear error.

summary: pause_recording / resume_recording creates multiple WAV chunks merged on stop — import path has no equivalent complexity
failure_scenario: Recording produces merged multi-chunk WAV; import is single file. If a feature needs to 'append' imported audio to an existing recording (or vice versa), the chunking/merging logic is duplicated or missing. No shared 'audio composition' abstraction.

summary: Recording flow: create_meeting → stop_recording → update_meeting (audioPath + durationSecs) — similar orphan window to old import flow
failure_scenario: If stop_recording succeeds but update_meeting fails, meeting exists with audio file on disk but no DB link. The import_meeting fix didn't address this symmetric case for recordings.

summary: UI import only via button click (open dialog) — no drag-drop handler wired to import_meeting
failure_scenario: If drag-drop is added, it must call import_meeting manually. The atomic command exists but the UI entry point is singular. Shared 'import from path' helper doesn't exist in frontend.

summary: Rollback on DB failure deletes copied file — but recording stop_recording doesn't clean up WAV on update_meeting failure
failure_scenario: Asymmetric cleanup: import is atomic with rollback; recording leaves orphan WAV if update_meeting fails after stop_recording. The fix depth stopped at import boundary.

summary: import_meeting_impl is pure function taking (sourcePath, title, db, audio_dir) — but audio_dir comes from AppState at call site
failure_scenario: Testability is good (pure function), but runtime call site in lib.rs passes state.audio_dir which assumes it exists. No validation that audio_dir is still accessible at call time — same as recorder.rs assumption.
